content.decision: leo tour --graph generates a self-contained HTML file with an interactive D3.js force-directed graph visualization of the memory graph.
content.context: Needed visual validation that landmark classification and tag co-occurrence make sense. Inspired by a reference project that produced similar HTML graph output.
content.why: Visual inspection is the fastest way to validate graph structure, landmark distribution, and tag clustering. A self-contained HTML requires no server or dependencies.
alternatives_considered: JSON export for external tools — adds friction, requires separate tooling
Terminal-based ASCII graph — too limited for hundreds of nodes
Web server with live data — overengineered for validation use case
impact: graph_html.go contains the D3.js template with MOM brand palette
BuildGraphData() in landmarks.go constructs the visualization payload
Clickable legend filters by document type (landmark, pattern, decision, learning, fact, feedback, other)
Logarithmic node sizing by edge count for visual differentiation
